streams.pump_role: single feed containing all reagents premixed in DES
streams.reasoning: From Chemistry Plan stream_logic: All components premixed in single stream. Phenylacetylene and ethyl nitroacetate are stable together at room temperature; nitrile oxide only forms upon heating. Premixing ensures dipolarophile is present to trap transient nitrile oxide immediately, suppressing dimerization to furoxan. Concentration adjusted from batch 0.5 M to 0.067 M based on calculation results for optimal residence time.

mixing_order_reasoning: From Chemistry Plan: Single premixed feed is optimal. Ethyl nitroacetate and phenylacetylene are mutually stable at ambient temperature. The nitrile oxide intermediate only forms at elevated temperature (≥100°C), so premixing before the heated reactor zone ensures immediate trapping of the 1,3-dipole by the alkyne, minimizing nitrile oxide dimerization.
pre_reactor_steps: Heat feed line to 50°C to reduce DES viscosity for improved pumping
post_reactor_steps: Cool reactor outlet to room temperature
Collect product for offline aqueous workup and DCM extraction
chemistry_notes: This is a thermal 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition proceeding via in-situ nitrile oxide generation from ethyl nitroacetate at 120°C, followed by [3+2] cycloaddition with phenylacetylene. The rate-limiting step is nitrile oxide formation, requiring precise temperature control. DES viscosity (~50 cP at RT) necessitates heated feed lines. The 60-minute residence time from analogies is much longer than the 15-minute batch time, suggesting mass transfer limitations in flow or different kinetics in the narrow reactor geometry. BPR is critical to prevent DES/EG boiling at 120°C.

reasoning_per_field.residence_time_min: Calculation estimated 60.0 min (range 1.0-90.0 min) from analogies. Analogy 3 used 20 min for [3+2] cycloaddition but at lower temperature (60°C vs 120°C). Analogy 2 used 179 min at 154°C but different chemistry. Using 60.0 min as it matches calculation and accounts for potential mass transfer limitations in narrow tubing with viscous DES.
reasoning_per_field.flow_rate_mL_min: Calculation: V_R/τ = 30.0 mL / 60.0 min = 0.5 mL/min. Verified against analogies: Analogy 3 used 0.165 mL/min, Analogy 2 used 0.12 mL/min - our 0.5 mL/min is reasonable for this scale.
reasoning_per_field.temperature_C: Same as batch (120°C) - required for nitrile oxide generation from ethyl nitroacetate. Chemistry Plan confirms this temperature is critical for the rate-limiting dehydration/tautomerization step.
reasoning_per_field.concentration_M: Calculation result: 0.067 M in reactor (diluted from batch 0.5 M). This accounts for the longer residence time needed in flow and ensures proper heat/mass transfer in narrow tubing.
reasoning_per_field.BPR_bar: Calculation: BPR REQUIRED at 5.0 bar. EG boiling point ~197°C, but DES mixture may have lower effective bp. BPR prevents solvent boiling and maintains liquid phase at 120°C operation.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_material: PFA chosen for high temperature operation (120°C) and chemical compatibility with DES. Not photochemistry so transparency not required. PFA handles temperature better than FEP.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_ID_mm: Calculation: 1.0 mm ID gives optimal heat transfer and reasonable pressure drop (0.13 bar). Matches calculation assumptions and provides good plug flow (Pe = 691).
reasoning_per_field.reactor_volume_mL: Calculation: 30.0 mL required for 60 min residence time at 0.5 mL/min flow rate. Verified: τ = V/Q = 30.0/0.5 = 60.0 min.

literature_analogies: Analogy 2: oxadiazole synthesis at high temperature
